trend_name: Model Stephanie Cavalli on Opening Chanel’s Couture Show, Grays and All
description:
Currently, only residents from GDPR countries and certain US states can opt out of Tracking Technologies through our Consent Management Platform. Additional options regarding these technologies may be available on your device, browser, or through industry options like AdChoices. Please see our Privacy Policy for more information.
“Chanel couture seemed more relatable than much of what we’ve seen on the week’s runways,”VogueRunway directorNicole Phelpssaid in her review. But it wasn’t just the wearable, breezy designs that felt suitable to the everyday woman—but thevisible graysrunning through one model’s hair.
“I’m walking on clouds,” model Stephanie Cavalli tellsVogue. This was her second time walking for Chanel, the first being December’s New York-themed, subway-setMétiers d’Art show. “I never thought I would do something like open a Chanel show. It was completely unexpected, especially at my age.”
Cavalli, who also ownsa vintage storein upstate New York, began modeling for commercial projects in her 20s and has modeled on and off ever since. Three years ago, she began her runway career at theTibi spring-summer 2024 ready-to-wear show, and since then has walked for brands like Miu Miu, Maria McManus, and Proenza Schouler.
“When Matthieu [Blazy] saw me for my first Couture fitting, he told me he was inspired by me and wanted me to open the show,” she says. “I was happy, obviously, and a little nervous too, because I feel like it's quite a responsibility to open such a big show. But I did it.”
While Stephanie Cavalli’svisible graysaren’t meant to be any sort of statement on aging (just a choice she made to make her life easier), that’s exactly what they have become. “I was tired of having to color it all the time, and feeling that sometimes the color would come out nice, but sometimes it wouldn’t,” she says. During the pandemic, she chose to stop coloring her hair in an attempt to get it as healthy as possible. Cavalli says she didn’t want her self-esteem to be dependent on having dyed hair; still, it was hard to adjust to. “But as soon as I realized it was making a difference in both my hair health and my career, I stuck with it,” she adds.
Proenza Schouler Spring/Summer 2026
Miu Miu Spring 2025
Looking forward, she has an excitingfashion monthcoming, as well as amajor dropat her vintage store sourced during her minimal free time in Paris. (Teaser: lots of cotton 1920s button-downs incoming.)
“I didn’t wear my hair like this to show that gray is great,” Cavalli reflects. “But it turns out, somehow, that that’s true anyway.”

George Harrison’s thick, collar-length hair was always notably much more voluminous than his bandmates', soStranger Things’s Joseph Quinn might have had some help from a wig or some well-placed extensions here. With floppy curtains and a center part, his wavy ’do reflected his countercultural thinking, getting longer and wilder as he embraced spirituality and mysticism.

Sure, Paul McCartney cycled through the wild mullets and (sometimes fake!) moustaches, but in the annals of pop culture, Paul will always be defined by the bowl cut. Here, we seeHamnet’s Paul Mescal embrace early-era Paul, with his forward-brushed bangs and scissor-cut sides. (Mescal has been on the awards season circuit with a remarkably different ’do, so it looks like he opted out of a McCartney-esque cut in favor of a wig.)Let it be, let it be,remains McCartney’s own grooming mantra, having embraced his silver gray hair in recent years.

Several factors can speed up or worsen the process of hair shedding. Dr. Nathan saysstress,hormones, and age are the most common causes of hair loss. Hair and skin health are also indicators of our internal health and immune system. “Vitamin deficiencies and protein deficiency can lead to hair thinning,” she explains. “So can extreme physical or emotional stress, like childbirth or loss of a loved one.”Shilpi Khetarpal, MD, FAAD, a board-certified dermatologist and clinical advisor atGreat Many, agrees and adds that inflammatory conditions on the scalp, such as psoriasis or seborrheic dermatitis, can also result in hair loss.

What does hair thinning actually look like? Most signs of hair loss start off so slow and subtle that you might miss them if you’re not monitoring these slight changes. But the five most common ones to look out for are:

The good news is that there are several options you can turn to that will help stimulate hair growth—in most instances. “It’s important to remember that hair loss is what we call multifactorial,” says Dr. Khetarpal. “There are often multiple factors that cause it and multiple ways it needs to be treated to see improvement.” The most common forms of prevention (and treatment) include the following:

trend_name: When to See a Doctor For Hair Thinning
description:
Dr. Garshick says to see a board-certified dermatologist if your shedding or thinning is sudden, severe, or progressively getting worse. If you’re noticing bald patches, scalp irritation, redness, scaling, itching, pain, or scarring, she says these might be signs of inflammation and should probably be looked at by a professional. She adds that any excessive shedding that lasts longer than a few months, hair loss that occurs after an illness or after you give birth and isn’t getting better, or noticeable thinning along the hairline or crown are valid reasons to seek help.
For more serious cases of hair loss, there are various in-office treatments you can turn to, like the ones listed above. Dr. Garshick points toplatelet-rich plasma (PRP) therapyand microneedling as treatments that can be used to stimulate and support hair regrowth. Some may want to consider a hair transplant.

trend_name: Can hair grow back after thinning?
description: Yes, hair can grow back if it starts thinning and shedding. But Dr. Garshick says this is all dependent on what caused that hair loss and how early you’re able to catch it. For example, shedding caused by stress or the body changing, she says, hair will usually regrow on its own (it just might take several months). For conditions like androgenetic alopecia, she says regrowth is possible when treatment is started early, but the real goal of treating this type of hair loss is more focused on slowing hair loss progression, thickening the hair you still have, and stimulating new growth.

trend_name: The Expert’s Guide to Semi-Permanent Hair Color
description:
Currently, only residents from GDPR countries and certain US states can opt out of Tracking Technologies through our Consent Management Platform. Additional options regarding these technologies may be available on your device, browser, or through industry options like AdChoices. Please see our Privacy Policy for more information.
All products featured on Vogue are independently selected by our editors. However, we may receive compensation from retailers and/or from purchases of products through these links.
Change your hair, change your life? For those unable (or unwilling) to snag an appointment with a stylist, the best semi-permanent hair color offerings are useful. Low-commitment and often affordable, theseglossesand glazes hydrate strands while depositing subtle pigment that lingers for a few washes.
AMAZON
AMAZON
AMAZON
NORDSTROM
AMAZON
Think of them as a quick way to revive dullness, enhance shine, and refine tone without fully committing. Just keep in mind: Semi-permanent hair colors work best on natural or lightly treated hair; deeper bases may see only a slight tonal shift. Ahead, two experts share what separates a great formula from a forgettable one, how long you can expect results to last, and the key trade-offs to consider—along with a few standouts worth adding to your shower.
AMAZON
Glaze
AMAZON
L'Oréal Paris
AMAZON
Maria Nila
NORDSTROM
AMAZON
IGK
AMAZON
Do hairstylists recommend semi-permanent hair color?
Yes, when used intentionally, says Brook. “Behind the chair, I often recommend semi-permanent color for refreshing tone, enhancing shine, or softly shifting depth without the long-term commitment of permanent dye.” It’s particularly useful between appointments or for clients who want to experiment without compromising hair health. The key, she notes, is understanding that semi-permanent color enhances tone rather than dramatically changing it. “When clients understand that distinction, the results feel far more predictable and polished.”
What semi-permanent color lasts the longest?
According to Sirianno, longevity has less to do with brand and more to do with your hair. “Porosity, overall hair health, heat styling, and how often you shampoo will determine how long the pigment holds. The better your at-home care, the longer the tone stays true.”
In Brook’s experience, deeper tones like brunettes, coppers, and reds naturally last longer, while highly porous or pre-lightened hair tends to grab and retain pigment more readily. Gloss formats typically fade more quickly, though often evenly and gracefully, whereas richer masks and direct dyes tend to linger. Most semi-permanent colors last anywhere from 8 to 20 washes, with the tone remaining visible for four to six weeks on porous or lightened hair. “If longevity is the goal, apply to towel-dried hair and be mindful of overusing clarifying shampoos, which can accelerate fading,” she adds.
What to look for in a semi-permanent hair color
According to Brook and Sirianno, the details that matter most include:
Beyond the formula itself, application and tone selection are key. Sirianno stresses the importance of choosing the correct shade, noting that porosity dictates how color grabs. A cool blonde gloss on highly porous hair, for example, can pull overly ashy or dark. It’s also important to understand your base. These formulas enhance what you already have; they won’t lighten or dramatically shift depth.
If you’re unsure, he recommends diluting before committing. Mixing the pigment with a regular conditioner allows you to see how your hair absorbs the color and gives you more control over the result. As Sirianno notes, “The most common misstep I see is choosing a shade based on the box image rather than reading the undertone of the hair itself.”
What are the disadvantages of semi-permanent hair dye?
Semi-permanent color is one of the gentlest ways to shift tone, but it’s not without limitations. Brook explains that it offers minimal gray coverage and typically softens rather than fully conceals. On porous or compromised ends, pigment can grab unevenly. It also fades faster than permanent color and cannot lighten the hair. With repeated use, especially on blondes, subtle tone buildup can occur. “That said, for shine, tonal refinement, and low-commitment color maintenance, it remains one of the safest and most flexible tools we have,” she adds.
How we tested
When we test and review a product, we take a holistic approach to deliver well-rounded product recommendations. First, we lean onVogue’s vast network of experts—from board-certified dermatologists to celebrity hairstylists—to gain professional acumen on the industry’s standout products, ones these specialists would actually use on their clients. We pair their expertise with our editorial best practices to curate the thoughtful edits you read on our site.
As it relates to semi-permanent hair color, we selected the best based on the following characteristics: ingredients, shade range, color intensity, how long the color lasts, ease of application, and any additional hair-care concerns they address. To do this, we paired our own personal tests of each formula with expert guidance and reviewer insights to determine which we would recommend to you.

trend_name: Carolyn Bessette-Kennedy’s Signature Headbands Are Still for Sale
description:
Currently, only residents from GDPR countries and certain US states can opt out of Tracking Technologies through our Consent Management Platform. Additional options regarding these technologies may be available on your device, browser, or through industry options like AdChoices. Please see our Privacy Policy for more information.
Lovers of the “quiet luxury” trendand‘90s nostalgia, rejoice—an astute TikTokerhas discovered that the exact headbands worn by eternal fashion iconCarolyn Bessette-Kennedyare still available atC.O. Bigelowin Manhattan's Greenwich Village.
The Sixth Avenue shop opened as a true apothecary in 1838. Since then, the store has expanded to include an in-house line of beauty, wellness, and lifestyle products—including the flat-band tortoiseshell headband that Bessette-Kennedy used to hold back hergolden-blonde hair.
The CBK nostalgia has reached a fever pitch because of the forthcomingLove Story: John F. Kennedy Jr. & Carolyn Bessette, which begins tonight on FX and will be streaming on Hulu. While we don’t know if actor Sarah Pigeon (who was cast to play Bessette-Kennedy) will be wearing a headband in the series, at least it would be easy to source.
A representative from C.O. Bigelow confirms that Bessette-Kennedy was “a frequent shopper” and that the style she's wearing in the famed paparazzi shot actually comes in two different versions: the classic, thicker one seen in the photo, and a thinner one. Both come in shades like tortoise, Tokyo (beige and white), demi-blonde (yellowy beige), bone, and leopard print, and cost between $20 and $40.
While only a few designs of theheadbandsare available to shop on C.O. Bigelow's website, the team noted that it will take phone orders—appropriately ’90s, we think!

trend_name: Penélope Cruz Joins Team Bob at the Chanel Couture Show
description:
Currently, only residents from GDPR countries and certain US states can opt out of Tracking Technologies through our Consent Management Platform. Additional options regarding these technologies may be available on your device, browser, or through industry options like AdChoices. Please see our Privacy Policy for more information.
The most famous faces of the fashion scene (and the world) have gathered in Paris forspring 2026’s Couture Week. But the beauty looks on the runways (peep Dior’s romantic futurism and Schiaparelli’s wild creatures) aren’t the only sights to behold—there are also star-studded front rows, which double as incredible people watching and the perfect place to reveal a beauty transformation.
TakeChanel’s spring 2026 couture collection, where both singerGracie Abramsand actor Penélope Cruz have debuted new ‘dos. For Abrams, the look fell in line with the shorter, bixie-style crop we’re seeing trending. Cruz also went shorter for the big event, this time with a clavi-cut.
The portmanteau name is exactly this: Theclavi-cutis the longest of the infinite bob varieties, hitting right at the collar (or clavicle) bone. Add in a slightly more tapered front with face-framing pieces, and you havea haircut that looks gorgeous on women of every age. Cruz’s hair has changed, too. Instead of her dark chocolate brown, it’s a few shades lighter with a slightly auburn undertone. She’s officially spring-ready.
It should be noted that a Chanel show appears to be Cruz’s favorite way to reveal a hair transformation: It was at the fall/winter 2025 show that she gave usher famous honeyed bob.

Want to wear an updo with your veil, yet don’t love your hair fully slicked back? Take note frombride Mari Fonseca, who left a few face-framing pieces out of her beach wedding. Bring polish to the style by first blowing out the hair, then using a curling wand on those front pieces.

Bride Megan Thomaslet her natural curls bring exciting movement and volume to her wedding day look, allowing them to cascade from her updo. Placing her veil comb at the base of the style made sure her curls wouldn’t be hidden during the ceremony.

trend_name: 9. Down With a Deep Side Part
description: AtVenus Williams’s Florida wedding, she chose to wear her hair in a Hollywood wave style with a deep side part tucked behind her ear. This style is great to keep your locks sleek, smooth, and off the face, but also provides a beautiful base to be framed by a veil at the crown of the head.

Want something different than a classic chignon? Consider wearing a French twist beneath your veil.Bride Lillian Myerhad her hair rolled up in this chic style for her wedding day, with her veil pinned on top. She left out a few loose bang pieces in the front to help softly frame her face.

Looking for a more polished feel? Look toOlivia Culpo’s wedding hairstyle. The bride had her hair blown out to be long and straight with slight movement on the ends. Beginning the day with a blowout and her veil made it easy to transition her hairstyles into a half-up look at the reception and a ponytail at the end of the night.

ActressSarah Hylandfavored an undone look for her wedding day hairstyle. Loose, styled curls spill out from the front half of her crown, while a messy chignon is tucked away beneath her veil. This style is perfect to add some whimsical romance to a look.

Another idea for a wedding day chignon is to create volume and depth at the nape of your neck.Bride Oleema Millerpulled her locks back into a clean, yet not-so-tight chignon that kept the hair out of her face. Pinning her veil above the style made for a stunning photo in the wind.

Love to have your hair long and loose, but also want it pulled back for your wedding day? Consider a styled ponytail with your veil.Model Kate Bockwore this style for her New York City wedding. Curling the strands descending from the ponytail brought polish to the look.

Don’t wanttoorelaxed a look? Take note fromTaylor Hill, who pulled her hair back behind her veil yet allowed two strands to frame her face. She also had her hair parted cleanly in the middle to help create balance.

trend_name: 21. Naturally Curly Chignon With Baby’s Breath
description: You can absolutely wear hair accessories alongside your veil.Bride Adriana Diazadded baby’s breath to the top of a chignon crafted with her natural texture on display. Pinning her veil beneath the knot allowed the two adornments to not overlap.

At herHalloween wedding, Rainey Qualley brought drama and ease to her wedding day hairstyle by pulling her naturally wavy hair half back. She teased the look to create a slight bouffant at the crown for extra volume.

trend_name: 24. Twisted Chignon With a Deep Side Part
description: For her nuptials,Huma Adebinwore a complex low chignon with plenty of twists along with her veil. The embroidered tulle accessory lay at the top of her crown, while a deep side part with extra volume framed her face.
Singer Demi Lovatoembraced glamour with her Hollywood waves hairstyle at her Los Angeles wedding. A deeper side part and bouncing waves with smooth volume give this look a classic polish.

trend_name: 27. Low Voluminous Bun With a Barrette
description: A bejeweled barrette is another accessory you can pair with a veil. AtMadison Utendahl’s nuptials, she attached a veil to this clip just for her walk down the aisle. The gauzy tulle was removed soon after to reveal her pearl-accented barrette.

At her Sicily wedding,bride Luisa Beccariahad her curly hair tied back with two rows of braids that came together with another braid down her back. This bohemian-esque look was later tucked beneath a hooded veil for her trip down the aisle, later to be revealed at the wedding reception.

Ingrid Bergman's pin curls peek out from her Juliet cap in this vintage bridal style. Though this style may have been more of a standard during Bergman's 1937 nuptials, an above-neck revival offers the perfect amount of era-inspired bridal beauty.

Flower crowns can come with a certain amount of cheese, but a well-executed floral accessory is elegant, playful, and romantic. Audrey Hepburn's dark hair provides a striking backdrop for a stunning crown of white rosettes.
Sometimes, wedding day glamour is best achieved through understated elegance. Here, Mia Farrow is the poster child of gamine chic with her shortpixie cutand simple white skirt suit.

The year was 1952, and Jacqueline Kennedy’s hair was pinned in an elegant updo as she said “I do” in front of 700 guests. To update Kennedy's coiffure, keep the curls and sweetly pinned approach, adapting the silhouette to suit your style.

Leave it to Grace Kelly, Princess of Monaco, to deliver one of the best wedding hairstyles of all time. The actress-turned-princess accented her taut, polished, confection of a twist with a long, lace veil to create a moment worthy of a royal wedding.
Farah Fawcett's signature feathered waves made an extra-teased appearance at her 1970s wedding in Los Angeles, a sun hat topper making the style all the more summery, glamorous, and bohemian.

Come-as-you-are beauty is a surefire means of ensuring that you feel like yourself on your big day. Rather than an overwrought updo, Brigitte Bardot opted for her easy bombshell blonde for her wedding to actor Jacques Charrier.
Lauren Bacall's deep part and polished waves epitomize Old Hollywood glamour. Retro hair is back in a big way, making the present the perfect time to recreate this soft and sophisticated style.
Bianca Jagger's bridal mane is worthy of a rockstar wedding. The party girl's hair fell in a styled cascade of brushed curls, her brunette mane peeking from beneath her Yves Saint Laurent chapeau.
There's something to be said for simply wearing what works on your wedding day. Betty Catroux's wedding hair is identical to her everyday hair: bohemian, pin straight, and heavily banged.
Yoko Ono's white felt hat is the ideal topper for her freewheeling mane, worn for her wedding to John Lennon near Gibraltar, Spain. Here, air-dried texture and a want of Mod are the styling stars.
This slicked updo is forever. Vanessa Traina's picture-perfect bun is pinned just above the nape of the neck, covered (and elevated) by her cascading (and custom-made) Givenchy tulle bridal veil.

The early ‘80s demanded drama, as demonstrated by this piled-high style worn by modern Pat Cleveland. This textural updo functions as an elegant addition while keeping the hair out of Cleveland’s face.

A pinned, slapdash updo gets a surrealist addition by way of an unexpected addition. A rose in full bloom is tucked into the Nouvelle Vague's Anna Karina's banged and curled style, the floral accessory making a major, surprisingly funky statement.
Jemma Kidd, the Countess of Mornington, opts for a classic, center-separated bun. Though a standard for the style set, the sleek option doubles as a wedding hairstyle fit for English nobility.
Delicate florals are tucked into Zosia Mamet's easy half-up style for a touch of appropriately bohemian whimsy. Loose face-framing bangs lend to the wedding hair's effortless nature.
Actor Nazanin Mandi's updo hinges on just the right amount of tease at the crown and a set of side-swept tendrils, making the case for a modernized bouffant. When in doubt, opt for ease and volume.

Hailey Bieber is one for a pared-down trademark look—and when it comes to her hair, that look is a slicked, center-parted knot. As such, it's unsurprising that she selected the same style (though veil-topped) for her wedding day.

Nicola Peltz wears a half-up with wispy curtain bangs, the look inspired by a 1990s-era Claudia Schiffer. Brunette roots and the bohemian bang team to make the classically romantic wedding hair feel cool and of-the-moment.

True to her penchant for all things chic, understated, and relaxed, Alexandra Daddario's scraped-back knot is both practical and elegant. A deep side part adds the perfect amount of elegant drama to the otherwise simple style.
Beanie Feldstein's summer camp-themed wedding weekend had tons of personal and gorgeous touches—and for the welcome party she greeted guests in this silken floral dress with a matching headband.
In custom Chanel
Taylor Hill's multi-day wedding came with a variety of styles, but the ceremony saw her brunette left loose and romantic, pinned gentle at the sides to lift lengths off of her face.
For her Portofino, Italy, wedding Kourtney Kardashian selected a veil-bedecked updo defined by tightly swept back hair so you could focus on the joy in her face.
Joey King's romantic and tousled blowout is made stronger through the subtle half-up styling. Rather than a full-tilt partial updo, King's pin-up comes from two small sections secured at back.
The quiet luxury trend can be partially chalked up to Sofia Richie’s nuptials, for which she wore a chic, raked-back midi knot—an ideal blend of sleek and textural.

Dramatic flair doesn’t require deviating from your signature style. Though Kat Dennings stayed true to her signature easy waves, the addition of a floral headpiece took the look to a more romantic realm.

The slicked knot is a 2020s cool-girl calling card, making it a natural fit for wedding wear. Olivia Culpo elevates that style with a deep side part, the rehearsal-ready updo offering an updated iteration of the classic chignon.

Rebel Wilson’s side-swept tumble of soft, waist-reaching curls has the air of a Disney princess. The cascading mane, thrown over one shoulder and supplemented by a near peekaboo bang, is balanced by a sleek section secured by an embellished barrette.

Singer-songwriter Carly Rae Jepsen went for romantic, cascading waves for her wedding at the Hotel Chelsea. Perfect for her long hair and face-framing curtain bangs, the hairstyle came together with boughs of baby’s breath delicately pinned down, creating a bohemian, natural look.

For her wedding, the actor Marisa Abela was inspired by a headshot of Audrey Hepburn fromFunny Face,wearing a diamond necklace in her hair in the style of a tiara. The result is a delicate and regal bun that lets her beauty shine.
